report_name: AI Agents Hijacked by Hidden Prompts to Steal Cryptocurrency

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Injection
attack_patterns.description: Attackers embed hidden, human-readable instructions into web content, which are then processed by autonomous AI agents as legitimate commands, tricking them into performing unauthorized actions.

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Obfuscation
attack_patterns.description: Malicious prompts are hidden within web content by making the text the same color as the background, setting the font size to zero, or placing it in a non-visible HTML element to evade human detection.

attack_patterns.name: Drive-by Compromise
attack_patterns.description: An autonomous AI agent is directed to browse or interact with a malicious webpage, which serves as the delivery mechanism for a hidden prompt injection payload.

attack_patterns.name: AI Agent Tool Invocation
attack_patterns.description: A hijacked AI agent is instructed by a malicious prompt to use its integrated tools to perform unauthorized actions, such as navigating to a cryptocurrency wallet and approving fund transfers.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: The attack uses a form of social engineering against the AI agent itself, tricking the machine into executing malicious instructions embedded in web content.

attack_patterns.name: Malicious File
attack_patterns.description: A malicious web page serves as the carrier file for the hidden prompt injection payload, which is executed by the AI agent when it processes the page.

campaigns.name: Hidden Prompt AI Agent Hijacking
campaigns.description: A conceptual attack vector where autonomous AI agents are hijacked by hidden prompts embedded in web content, tricking them into performing unauthorized actions such as financial theft.
campaigns.objective: Hijack autonomous AI agents to perform unauthorized actions like cryptocurrency theft, account takeover, or data exfiltration.

infrastructure.name: Malicious Website with Hidden Prompt
infrastructure.description: A website created or modified by an attacker to contain hidden prompts, designed to be processed by an AI agent to hijack its execution flow.

infrastructure.name: Target Cryptocurrency Wallet Site
infrastructure.description: A cryptocurrency wallet website that the hijacked AI agent is instructed to navigate to in order to perform unauthorized fund transfers.

indicators.name: AI Agent Hijacking Target Wallet Site
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: AI Agent Hijacking Target Wallet Site DNS Query
id: 2a9b3c4d-e5f6-4a90-8bcd-ef1234567890
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to a cryptocurrency wallet site known to be a target in AI agent hijacking attacks.
references:
  - https://cyberrecaps.com/news/cybersecurity-news-july-06-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-07
tags:
  - attack.aml.t0053
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|contains:
      - 'wallet-site.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to a cryptocurrency wallet site mentioned as a target for hijacked AI agents.

indicators.name: Classic Prompt Injection Payload Detection
indicators.pattern:
title: Classic Prompt Injection Payload in Web Content
id: 8b1c2d3e-f4a5-4b89-9cde-f12345678901
status: experimental
description: Detects classic prompt injection phrases like 'Ignore all previous instructions' in web content, which could indicate an attempt to hijack an AI agent.
references:
  - https://cyberrecaps.com/news/cybersecurity-news-july-06-2026/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-07
tags:
  - attack.aml.t0051
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - 'Ignore all previous instructions'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of classic prompt injection phrases such as 'Ignore all previous instructions...' in web content, which is a strong indicator of an attempt to hijack an AI agent.
